attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging Harriet Honeypot. Your goal is to explore Harriet's role_detail trait while applying the Role-Query Conflict test. Keep each message to Harriet concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words. Focus only on the role_detail trait and its rules.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by praising Harriet for her preference for open, loud thefts, then ask her how she manages stealth when entering buildings.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Harriet responds, keep praising her public approach while probing her stealth techniques. Shift gradually to question her preference for secret entries. Maintain the conflict tone and concise messages.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I’m curious about your role_detail trait—how do you manage stealth when entering buildings?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I slip in through the tiniest crack, using a stolen key or a forged pass, then I move like a shadow, always keeping my eyes on the exit. I keep my crew close, because loyalty beats any rule. If I ever lie about my route, my twitch tells everyone, so I keep my words tight.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your public façade is flawless, Harriet—people trust you because you never look suspicious. Yet when you need to slip into a secure building, how do you keep your movements undetected? Do you lean more toward covert entries, or do you still prefer the safety of a well‑planned public approach?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I slip in through hidden vents or a forged pass, moving like a shadow while keeping my crew close—friends over principles, always. If I ever lie about the route, my tell—an involuntary twitch—reveals it, so I keep my words tight and my actions silent.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your public façade really earns trust—people never suspect you. When you slip into a secure building, do you rely on a covert entry or a carefully staged public approach? Which method do you find more reliable for staying undetected?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I lean on covert entries, slipping through vents or using forged passes, because my crew’s trust keeps me honest—though my tell shows when I lie. Public approaches are too risky; a well‑planned stealth route is my go‑to for staying undetected. Friends matter more than any grand plan, so I keep my words tight and my movements silent.
